sourceQuestion:
In an IPv4 datagram, the MF bit is 0, the value of HLEN is 10, the value of total length is 400 and the fragment offset value is 300. The position of the datagram, the sequence numbers of the first and the last bytes of the payload, respectively are:
A. Last fragment, 2400 and 2759
B. First fragment, 2400 and 2759
C. Last fragment, 2400 and 2759
D. Middle fragment, 300 and 669

parsed.stem: In an IPv4 datagram, the MF bit is 0, the value of HLEN is 10, the value of total length is 400 and the fragment offset value is 300. The position of the datagram, the sequence numbers of the first and the last bytes of the payload, respectively are:

sourceQuestion:
Suppose in a web browser, you click on the URL www.gate2023.in. The browser cache is empty. The IP address for this URL is not cached in your local host, so a DNS lookup is triggered (by the local DNS server deployed on your local host) over the 3-tier hierarchy in an iterative mode. No resource records are cached anywhere across all servers in the DNS hierarchy. Also, the round trip time between the local host and the web server hosting www.gate2023.in is also equal to RTT. The HTML file associated with www.gate2023.in is small enough to have negligible transmission time. Neglect rendering time by your web browser, which references 10 equally small objects on the same web server.

Which of the following statements is/are CORRECT about the minimum elapsed time between clicking on the URL and the browser fully rendering it?
A. 7 RTTs, in case of non-persistent HTTP with 5 parallel TCP connections.
B. 5 RTTs, in case of persistent HTTP with pipelining.
C. 5 RTTs, in case of non-persistent HTTP with 5 parallel TCP connections.
D. 6 RTTs, in case of persistent HTTP with pipelining.
sourceAnswer: D. 6 RTTs, in case of persistent HTTP with pipelining.

parsed.stem:
Suppose in a web browser, you click on the URL www.gate2023.in. The browser cache is empty. The IP address for this URL is not cached in your local host, so a DNS lookup is triggered (by the local DNS server deployed on your local host) over the 3-tier hierarchy in an iterative mode. No resource records are cached anywhere across all servers in the DNS hierarchy. Also, the round trip time between the local host and the web server hosting www.gate2023.in is also equal to RTT. The HTML file associated with www.gate2023.in is small enough to have negligible transmission time. Neglect rendering time by your web browser, which references 10 equally small objects on the same web server.

Which of the following statements is/are CORRECT about the minimum elapsed time between clicking on the URL and the browser fully rendering it?

options.text: 7 RTTs, in case of non-persistent HTTP with 5 parallel TCP connections.
5 RTTs, in case of persistent HTTP with pipelining.
5 RTTs, in case of non-persistent HTTP with 5 parallel TCP connections.
6 RTTs, in case of persistent HTTP with pipelining.

sourceQuestion:
Which one of the following prefixes exactly represents the range of IP addresses 10.12.0.0 to 10.12.3.255?
A. 10.12.0.0/23
B. 10.12.0.0/22
C. 10.12.0.0/21
D. 10.12.0.0/24

parsed.stem: Which one of the following prefixes exactly represents the range of IP addresses 10.12.0.0 to 10.12.3.255?

sourceQuestion:
A machine receives an IPv4 datagram. The protocol field of the IP header has the protocol number of a protocol X. Which ONE of the following is NOT a possible candidate for X?
A. Internet Control Message Protocol (ICMP)
B. Internet Group Management Protocol (IGMP)
C. Open Shortest Path First (OSPF)
D. Routing Information Protocol (RIP)

parsed.stem: A machine receives an IPv4 datagram. The protocol field of the IP header has the protocol number of a protocol X. Which ONE of the following is NOT a possible candidate for X?

sourceQuestion:
For which one of the following reasons does Internet Protocol (IP) use the Time-to-Live (TTL) field in the IP datagram header?
A. Ensure packets reach destination within that time
B. Discard packets that reach later than that time
C. Prevent packets from looping indefinitely
D. Limit the time for which a packet queues at intermediate routers
sourceAnswer: C. Prevent packets from looping indefinitely

parsed.stem: For which one of the following reasons does Internet Protocol (IP) use the Time-to-Live (TTL) field in the IP datagram header?

options.text: Ensure packets reach destination within that time
Discard packets that reach later than that time
Limit the time for which a packet queues at intermediate routers

sourceQuestion:
One of the header fields in an IP datagram is the Time-to-Live (TTL) field. Which of the following statements best explains the need for this field?
A. It can be used to prioritize packets.
B. It can be used to reduce delays.
C. It can be used to optimize throughput.
D. It can be used to prevent packets from looping indefinitely.
sourceAnswer: D. It can be used to prevent packets from looping indefinitely.

parsed.stem: One of the header fields in an IP datagram is the Time-to-Live (TTL) field. Which of the following statements best explains the need for this field?

options.text: It can be used to prioritize packets.
It can be used to reduce delays.
It can be used to optimize throughput.
It can be used to prevent packets from looping indefinitely.

sourceQuestion:
An IP router with a Maximum Transmission Unit (MTU) of 1500 bytes has received an IP packet of size 4444 bytes with an IP header of length 20 bytes. The values of the relevant fields in the header of the third fragment generated by the router for this packet are:

A. MF: 0, Datagram Length: 1444; Offset: 370
B. MF: 1, Datagram Length: 1424; Offset: 185
C. MF: 0, Datagram Length: 1500; Offset: 370
D. MF: 0, Datagram Length: 1444; Offset: 2960

sourceQuestion:
Which of the following fields of an IP header is NOT modified by a typical IP router?

A. Checksum
B. Source address
C. Time to Live (TTL)
D. Length

parsed.stem: Which of the following fields of an IP header is NOT modified by a typical IP router?

sourceQuestion:
Host A sends a datagram containing 8880 bytes of user data to Host B over an Ethernet LAN. Ethernet frames may carry data up to 1500 bytes (i.e., MTU = 1500 bytes). Size of IP header is 20 bytes and size of UDP header is 8 bytes. There is no option field in the IP header. How many total number of fragments will be transmitted and what will be the contents of the offset field in the last fragment?

A. 6 and 7400
B. 7 and 1110
C. 6 and 925
D. 7 and 8880

parsed.stem: Host A sends a datagram containing 8880 bytes of user data to Host B over an Ethernet LAN. Ethernet frames may carry data up to 1500 bytes (i.e., MTU = 1500 bytes). How many total number of fragments will be transmitted and what will be the contents of the offset field in the last fragment?

sourceQuestion:
Which of the following fields is/are modified in the header of a packet going out of a network address translation (NAT) device from an internal network to an external network?

A. Source IP
B. Destination IP
C. Header Checksum
D. Total Length

parsed.stem: Which of the following fields is/are modified in the header of a packet going out of a network address translation (NAT) device from an internal network to an external network?

sourceQuestion:
Which of the following statements about IPv4 fragmentation is/are TRUE?

A. The fragmentation of an IP datagram is performed only at the source of the datagram
B. The fragmentation of an IP datagram is performed at any router which finds that the size of the datagram to be transmitted exceeds the MTU
C. The reassembly of IP fragments is performed only at the destination of the datagram
D. The reassembly of IP fragments is performed at all intermediate routers along the path from the source to the destination

parsed.stem: Which of the following statements about IPv4 fragmentation is/are TRUE?

options.text: The fragmentation of an IP datagram is performed only at the source of the datagram
The fragmentation of an IP datagram is performed at any router which finds that the size of the datagram to be transmitted exceeds the MTU
The reassembly of IP fragments is performed only at the destination of the datagram
The reassembly of IP fragments is performed at all intermediate routers along the path from the source to the destination

sourceQuestion:
Which of the following fields of an IP header is/are always modified by any router before it forwards the IP packet?
A. Source Address
B. Protocol
C. Time to Live (TTL)
D. Header Checksum

parsed.stem: Which of the following fields of an IP header is/are always modified by any router before it forwards the IP packet?

sourceQuestion:

A. Fragment Offset
B. Source IP address
C. Destination IP address
D. Destination port number

sourceQuestion:
A 2 km long broadcast LAN has 10^8 bps bandwidth and uses CSMA/CD. The signal travels along the wire at 2*10^8 m/s. What is the minimum packet size that can be used on this network?
A. 50 bytes
B. 100 bytes
C. 200 bytes
D. None of the above

sourceQuestion:
There are n stations in a slotted LAN. Each station attempts to transmit with a probability p in each time slot. What is the probability that ONLY one station transmits in a given time slot?
A. np(1-p)^(n-1)
B. (1-p)^(n-1)
C. p(1-p)^(n-1)
D. 1-(1-p)^(n-1)

parsed.stem: There are n stations in a slotted LAN. Each station attempts to transmit with a probability p in each time slot. What is the probability that ONLY one station transmits in a given time slot?

sourceQuestion:
In the diagram shown below, L1 is an Ethernet LAN and L2 is a Token-Ring LAN. An IP packet originates from sender S and traverses to R, as shown. The links within each LAN and across the two LANs, are all point-to-point optical links. The initial value of the TTL field is 32. The maximum possible value of the TTL field when R receives the datagram is ___.
A. 29
B. 30
C. 31
D. 32

The maximum possible value of the TTL field when R receives the datagram is ___.

sourceQuestion:
A host is connected to a Department network which is part of a University network. The University network, in turn, is part of the Internet. The largest network in which the Ethernet address of the host is unique is
A. the subnet to which the host belongs
B. the Department network
C. the University network
D. the Internet

parsed.stem: A host is connected to a Department network which is part of a University network. The University network, in turn, is part of the Internet. The largest network in which the Ethernet address of the host is unique is

options.text: the subnet to which the host belongs

sourceQuestion:
Which of the following statements is FALSE regarding a bridge?
A. Bridge is a layer 2 device
B. Bridge reduces collision domain
C. Bridge is used to connect two or more LAN segments
D. Bridge reduces broadcast domain

parsed.stem: Which of the following statements is FALSE regarding a bridge?

options.text: Bridge is used to connect two or more LAN segments

A. 0.01
B. 3.36
C. 6.72
D. 8

A. 94
B. 416
C. 464
D. 512

sourceQuestion:
Consider a simplified time slotted MAC protocol, where each host always has data to send and transmits with probability p = 0.5 in every slot. There is no backoff and one frame can be transmitted in one slot. If more than one host transmits in the same slot, then the transmissions are unsuccessful due to collision. What is the maximum number of hosts that this protocol can support if we want the probability that a given slot has a successful transmission to be at least 2/3?

A. 1  B. 2  C. 3  D. 4

parsed.stem: Consider a simplified time slotted MAC protocol, where each host always has data to send and transmits with probability p = 0.5 in every slot. There is no backoff and one frame can be transmitted in one slot. If more than one host transmits in the same slot, then the transmissions are unsuccessful due to collision. What is the maximum number of hosts that this protocol can support if we want the probability that a given slot has a successful transmission to be at least 2/3?

sourceQuestion:
In a TDM medium access control bus LAN, each station is assigned one time slot per cycle for transmission. Assume that the length of each time slot is the time to transmit 100 bits plus the end-to-end propagation delay. Assume a propagation speed of 2 * 10^8 m/sec. The length of the LAN is 1 km with a bandwidth of 10 Mbps. The maximum number of stations that the LAN can support is:

A. 3  B. 5  C. 10  D. 20

The length of the LAN is 1 km with a bandwidth of 10 Mbps. The maximum number of stations that the LAN can support is:

sourceQuestion:
Consider two hosts X and Y connected by a single direct link of rate 10^8 bits/sec. The distance between the two hosts is 10,000 km and the propagation speed along the link is 2 * 10^8 m/sec. Host X sends a message of 50,000 bytes to host Y continuously as one large message. Let the transmission and propagation delays be p milliseconds and q milliseconds respectively. Then the value of p and q are:

A. p = 50 and q = 100    B. p = 50 and q = 400    C. p = 100 and q = 50    D. p = 800 and q = 50

Let the transmission and propagation delays be p milliseconds and q milliseconds respectively. Then the value of p and q are:

sourceQuestion:
A TCP message consisting of 2100 bytes of data is passed to IP for delivery across two networks. The first network can carry a maximum payload of 1200 bytes per frame and the second network can carry a maximum payload of 400 bytes per frame, excluding network overhead. Assume that IP overhead per packet is 20 bytes. What is the total IP overhead for transmitting the message across the two networks?

A. 40 bytes
B. 80 bytes
C. 120 bytes
D. 160 bytes

parsed.stem: A TCP message consisting of 2100 bytes of data is passed to IP for delivery across two networks. The first network can carry a maximum payload of 1200 bytes per frame and the second network can carry a maximum payload of 400 bytes per frame, excluding network overhead. Assume that IP overhead per packet is 20 bytes. What is the total IP overhead for transmitting the message across the two networks?

sourceQuestion:
A link of capacity 100 Mbps is carrying traffic from a number of sources. Each source generates an on-off traffic stream; when the source is on, the rate of traffic is 2 Mbps, and when the source is off, the rate of traffic is zero. The duty cycle, which is the ratio of on-time to (on-time + off-time), is 1/2. When there is no buffering, loss occurs when the combined traffic from all sources exceeds the link capacity. Assuming that all sources are synchronized (they all turn on and off at the same time), the minimum number of sources (S1) that can be multiplexed on the link without loss, and assuming that all sources are NOT synchronized (with a large buffer), the maximum number of sources (S2) that can be multiplexed on the link so that data loss probability is zero, are:

A. 10 and 30
B. 12 and 25
C. 5 and 33
D. 15 and 22

parsed.stem: A link of capacity 100 Mbps is carrying traffic from a number of sources. Each source generates an on-off traffic stream; when the source is on, the rate of traffic is 2 Mbps, and when the source is off, the rate of traffic is zero. The duty cycle, which is the ratio of on-time to (on-time + off-time), is 1/2. When there is no buffering, loss occurs when the combined traffic from all sources exceeds the link capacity. Assuming that all sources are synchronized (they all turn on and off at the same time), the minimum number of sources (S1) that can be multiplexed on the link without loss, and assuming that all sources are NOT synchronized (with a large buffer), the maximum number of sources (S2) that can be multiplexed on the link so that data loss probability is zero, are:

sourceQuestion:
Which of the following functionality must be implemented by a transport protocol over and above the network protocol?

A. Recovery from packet losses
B. Detection of duplicate packets
C. Packet delivery in the correct order
D. End to end connectivity

parsed.stem: Which of the following functionality must be implemented by a transport protocol over and above the network protocol?

options.text: Packet delivery in the correct order

sourceQuestion:
Choose the best matching between Group-1 and Group-2:

Group-1:
P. Data link layer
Q. Network layer
R. Transport layer

Group-2:
1. Ensures reliable transport of data over a physical point-to-point link
2. Encodes/decodes data for physical transmission
3. Allows end-to-end communication between two processes
4. Routes data from one network node to the next

A. P-1, Q-4, R-3
B. P-2, Q-4, R-1
C. P-2, Q-3, R-1
D. P-1, Q-3, R-2

parsed.stem:
Choose the best matching between Group-1 and Group-2:

Group-1:
P. Data link layer
Q. Network layer
R. Transport layer

Group-2:
1. Ensures reliable transport of data over a physical point-to-point link
2. Encodes/decodes data for physical transmission
3. Allows end-to-end communication between two processes
4. Routes data from one network node to the next

sourceQuestion:
Assume that source S and destination D are connected through two intermediate routers labeled R. Determine how many times each packet has to visit the network layer and the data link layer during a transmission from S to D.

A. Network layer - 4 times and Data link layer - 4 times
B. Network layer - 4 times and Data link layer - 3 times
C. Network layer - 4 times and Data link layer - 6 times
D. Network layer - 2 times and Data link layer - 6 times
sourceAnswer: C. Network layer - 4 times and Data link layer - 6 times

parsed.stem: Assume that source S and destination D are connected through two intermediate routers labeled R. Determine how many times each packet has to visit the network layer and the data link layer during a transmission from S to D.

options.text: Network layer - 4 times and Data link layer - 4 times
Network layer - 4 times and Data link layer - 3 times
Network layer - 4 times and Data link layer - 6 times
Network layer - 2 times and Data link layer - 6 times

sourceQuestion:
In the following pairs of OSI protocol layer/sub-layer and its functionality, the INCORRECT pair is:

A. Network layer and Routing
B. Data Link Layer and Bit synchronization
C. Transport layer and End-to-end process communication
D. Medium Access Control sub-layer and Channel sharing
sourceAnswer: B. Data Link Layer and Bit synchronization

parsed.stem: In the following pairs of OSI protocol layer/sub-layer and its functionality, the INCORRECT pair is:

options.text: Data Link Layer and Bit synchronization
Transport layer and End-to-end process communication
Medium Access Control sub-layer and Channel sharing
